overview: Magic: The Gathering — Final Fantasy invites players to explore iconic locations and characters from the legendary JRPG franchise. The format is built around classic mechanics like Landfall, Flashback, and a heavy focus on Artifact and Equipment synergies.

mechanics.name: Landfall
mechanics.description: Triggers whenever a land enters the battlefield under your control.
mechanics.draft_tip: Look for ways to play extra lands to strengthen your creatures even further.

mechanics.name: Flashback
mechanics.description: Allows you to cast spells from your graveyard to crush foes under the weight of the past.
mechanics.draft_tip: Mill cards early to fill your graveyard with powerful creatures and flashback spells, then bring them back to achieve victory.

mechanics.name: Wizard Tokens
mechanics.description: Tokens created by certain spells and creatures to build a barrage of damage.
mechanics.draft_tip: Draft cards that create Wizard tokens to increase your high creature count for hard-hitting spells.

archetypes.name: Artifacts
archetypes.description: Build your arsenal by playing artifacts, including artifact creatures and token-generating equipment, then gain extra bonuses for playing those artifacts.
archetypes.strategy: Synergize artifact creatures with 'Artifacts Matter' payoffs to grind out value.

archetypes.name: Control
archetypes.description: Dominate the long game with strategies like removal spells, card draw, and tricky creatures. Cast flashback spells to crush foes under the weight of the past.
archetypes.strategy: Play reactively, using removal and card draw to reach the late game where your flashback value takes over.

archetypes.name: Black Mage Aggro
archetypes.description: Play the aggressive game with creatures that reward you whenever you cast a noncreature spell. Look for cards that create Wizard tokens and turn those spells into a barrage of damage.
archetypes.strategy: Curve out with 'spells-matter' creatures and use burn to clear the path for your Wizard tokens.

archetypes.name: Landfall Aggro
archetypes.description: Play creatures with landfall abilities, like Chocobos, and gain bonuses as you play lands. Look for ways to play extra lands and strengthen your creatures even further.
archetypes.strategy: Focus on high-pressure creatures that get massive during your turn when you drop a land.

archetypes.name: Go Wide
archetypes.description: Call forth battalions of formidable fighters and play cards that make tokens. Reinforce your standing army or leverage your high creature count to cast hard-hitting spells.
archetypes.strategy: Swarm the board with tokens and use anthem effects or 'creature-count' spells to finish the game.

archetypes.name: Artifact & Creature Sacrifice
archetypes.description: Sacrifice your artifacts and creatures, especially ones with death effects, to steadily gain the upper hand. Token-generating equipment gives you two things to sacrifice.
archetypes.strategy: Use disposable tokens to trigger death-based value engines and out-grind the opponent.

archetypes.name: Big Noncreatures
archetypes.description: Whenever you cast spells that cost 4 or more mana, you’ll gain bonuses in battle. Find ways to generate tokens, like with special equipment, so that your noncreature spells can add to your forces.
archetypes.strategy: Ramp into high-CMC spells that provide massive board impact while keeping your creature count high through tokens.

archetypes.name: Graveyard Value
archetypes.description: Slow and steady wins the game! Mill to fill your graveyard early with powerful creatures and flashback spells, then bring them back to achieve victory.
archetypes.strategy: Treat your graveyard as a second hand. Use recursion to replay your best threats repeatedly.

archetypes.name: Equipment Aggro
archetypes.description: Grab your favorite iconic weapon and rush into battle! Look for creatures that want to be equipped or cards that reward you for having Equipment, then gear up to outclass your opponent.
archetypes.strategy: Aggressively draft equipment. A single creature with a powerful weapon can often win the game on its own.

archetypes.name: Town Ramp
archetypes.description: Travel the crossroad Towns! Towns are lands that grant more perks the more of them you have. It makes a bunch and reap the rewards! You'll even want to play some Towns that aren't blue or green; make sure to splash in some other colors, too!
archetypes.strategy: Prioritize Town lands highly. This is a multi-color ramp strategy that wins through superior mana and land-based utility.

general_tips: Towns are the cornerstone of the Green-Blue strategy; splash other colors freely if you have 3+ Towns.
In Red-White, draft creatures that specifically reward being equipped to maximize your Equipment Aggro synergy.
Flashback spells effectively double your card advantage in long games, making UB Control very formidable.
Don't be afraid to sacrifice tokens in WB; token-generating equipment is specifically designed to provide fodder.
Landfall triggers only on your turn usually—timing your land drops is critical for the RG Aggro archetype.
